content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Apr 4, 2026)
## OVERVIEW 
The day saw no new code contributions, but significant discussion emerged around agent security and identity with new plugin proposals for capability token enforcement and pre-trade safety checks.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

No new technical developments were completed today.

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
No new pull requests were opened today.

## CLOSED ISSUES

No issues were closed today.

## NEW ISSUES

### Agent Security and Authorization Proposals
Two new issues were opened proposing plugins to enhance agent security and authorization. Issue [#6707](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6707) proposes a `@sint/eliza-plugin` for capability token enforcement on Eliza agent tool calls, aiming to introduce a formal authorization layer for actions like blockchain transactions or file operations. Issue [#6706](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6706) suggests a `SafeAgent` plugin to perform token safety checks (honeypot, scam, rug pull detection) before crypto trading.

## ACTIVE ISSUES

### Agent Identity and Trust Layer Discussion
Discussion continued on issue [#6688](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6688), which proposes an `AgentID` plugin for cryptographic identity and trust layers for ElizaOS agents. A detailed comment from pshkv highlighted the overlap with SINT Protocol's Avatar Layer, specifically its `AvatarProfile` and `CSML (Composite Safety-Model Latency)` for behavioral fingerprinting. The comment also outlined key differences, such as AgentID's focus on Solana wallet addresses as identity and blockchain receipts, and suggested aligning behavioral fingerprinting approaches for compatibility. A follow-up comment from haroldmalikfrimpong-ops clarified that AgentID's trust levels are capability-based and dynamic, with real-time adjustments based on behavioral baselines, and emphasized the server-side auto-detection of anomalies.
